evidence_quotes.quote: fine print clauses that state they will only guaranty availability for 5 years

paragraphs.text: Video games or other digital goods that have been sold with the words "Buy" or "Purchase" should be made available for download and offline use in working condition. Hundreds of games have been lost due to publishers pulling the availability of games offline due to purposely designed reliance on servers. Purchased digital goods like books can be taken away on services like Amazon kindle or other publishing sites while these can easily be made available in pdf format to be downloaded. On these sites are fine print clauses that state they will only guaranty availability for 5 years, while clearly stating to "Buy" or "Purchase" an item. It should not be up to them to take these things away from us. And piracy is a bad excuse to screw over paying customers. The pirates have it better than paying customers. They get to own what they didn't pay for. Customers can not.
